title: allium splendens subsp. insulare
intro: Allium splendens subsp. insulare, also known as the Corsican onion or island ornamental onion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and edible leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as an antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agent, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Allium splendens subsp. insulare include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, while its herbal preparations typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the plant's leaves and flowers. Additionally, the plant's bulbs can be used in cooking, adding a mild onion flavor to various dishes, and its leaves can be used as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
